title: Ukraine's arms makers fear slowdown after defense minister ousted
excerpt: Defense industry sources worry about what Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal could mean for battlefield innovation at a pivotal moment in the war.
content_html:
Ukraine's weapons makers fear a costly slowdown after their tech champion was pushed out of the defense ministry

By Jake Epstein

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

and Sinéad Baker

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise dismissal of his reformist defense minister has sent shockwaves through the country, causing unease and anxiety as Kyiv enjoyed a wave of fresh momentum on the battlefield.

Ukrainian defense industry sources described the removal of 35-year-old Mykhailo Fedorov last week, after just six months on the job, as a major disappointment for the country's powerhouse military-technology sector, expressing concerns about serious problems down the road.

A Ukrainian defense tech company representative who asked only to be identified as Anton said that Fedorov "was driving innovations, visionary goals, and very brave practical operations that gave President Zelenskyy his cards."

Anton said that Fedorov was proactive rather than reactive in his efforts to substantially overhaul Ukrainian weapons development, tactics, and doctrine, adding that "he was fulfilling today's needs and gaps while building tomorrow's relevance and superiority to ensure our victory."

A reformist approach to a grinding war

Fedorov, who previously served as Ukraine's minister for digital transformation and is known for his Silicon Valley-style approach to the war, was appointed by Zelenskyy in January to oversee the defense ministry.

He quickly pushed to dramatically reform Ukraine's military, including placing greater emphasis on deploying drones and reshaping the contracting system. Fedorov also sought to overhaul the defense industry to increase development and make the procurement process more transparent and efficient.

Russia's invasion has turned Ukraine into a central hub for robotics and drone innovation. Hundreds of companies are building new technologies — from drones to electronic warfare devices — for the military, and many have their eyes set on future exports.

During Fedorov's tenure, Ukrainian firms enjoyed a much more transparent procurement process with the government, Anton said. He described collaborating with the defense ministry as a "professional and very productive" dialogue, "something we never could imagine before."

He said that Fedorov transformed the defense industry to meet the challenges of a changing battlefield, one that is becoming increasingly autonomous.

Since January, Ukrainian forces have been significantly ramping up their long-range drone strikes on Russian oil facilities and have used mid-range drones to hammer Russian logistics in the rear. The two campaigns have given Kyiv a sense of optimism and stymied Moscow's battlefield momentum while fueling its own.

Defense manufacturers see Ukraine's victories over the past few months as a testament to the drone-forward approach of Fedorov and his team.

The CEO of robotic systems maker Ark Robotics said that "policies supporting rapid deployment, streamlined procurement, and continuous battlefield feedback created the conditions for rapid technological progress."

Losing the pace of innovation

Fedorov's emphasis on rapid technological change and decentralized innovation sometimes clashed with the military's more conventional command structure under Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi. Zelenskyy said the rift between the two had become a problem.

Zelenskyy omitted Fedorov from his government in a major reshuffle last week. The former defense minister said he was pushed out, and while he declined a government advisory role, it's unclear whether he could stay on in another capacity.

The dismissal caused confusion and anger among Ukrainian civilians and soldiers, and has stoked anxiety within the defense industry over the future of procurement and manufacturing priorities.

As defense minister, "Fedorov built a system where only equipment that actually worked and delivered results made it to the front," a Ukrainian manufacturer said, adding that "he created a competitive environment: manufacturers competed on quality, lowered their prices, and still met the front's real needs."

They said they worry about a potential backslide to "manual, top-down control."

Another Ukrainian manufacturer said they are "deeply concerned" as a producer, not only because of Fedorov's dismissal but also because Kyiv has gone through three defense ministers in a little over a year.

"This creates an unpredictable frame for work," they said. "Changing a minister and his team usually takes time to adjust all processes. This means that the adoption of necessary decisions can be slower for some time. Thus, this can affect our work as well."

Zelenskyy said that he asked Maj. Gen. Yevhen Khmara, the acting head of Ukraine's SBU internal security agency, to lead the defense ministry in the interim.

Khmara previously led the SBU's elite Alpha unit, which has been the tip of the spear for Ukraine's deep strikes into Russia, so drone innovation may remain a priority for the defense ministry.

The Ark Robotics CEO said it remains to be seen whether Ukraine has the "institutional, strategic commitment to realize the same vision going forward and accelerate the changes" that Fedorov started.

Proper policies will enable defense innovation to move at the rapid pace required in a modern war, they said. "Our take is that being radical in implementing widespread use of unmanned systems is an existential question for Ukraine."

Without Fedorov at the helm, Ukrainian defense manufacturers don't see their efforts completely collapsing. However, they are worried about a potential change in pace that could prove harmful at a pivotal moment in the war.

"What happens to the industry, army, and country without Minister Fedorov?" Anton said. "Heaven will not fall on earth; the industry will work, as we all have a strong motivation to win this war, or at least not to lose."

"We will continue doing our best, as well as the guys in the trenches," he said, but there's a risk that "we will lose the pace of development."

Sinéad Baker

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Sinéad Baker is a Military and Defense Correspondent based in Business Insider's London bureau, writing about Russia's invasion of Ukraine and NATO actions.Sinéad most often covers soldiers' experiences, military strategy, battlefield developments, the defense industry's response, and geopolitical decisions that surround the war. She has reported from NATO’s frontlines and around Europe,  has interviewed multiple prime ministers and defense ministers, has appeared on BBC News and The Guardian's politics podcast, and has been cited by Congressional hearings.Sinéad has also extensively covered US politics and previously led Business Insider's breaking news coverage from London.Sinéad previously completed a master's degree in investigative journalism at City, University of London, and has written for The Guardian, The Observer, and TheJournal.ie. Sinéad is the former editor of the multi-award-winning The University Times in Dublin.Expertise

  • Experiences of soldiers in Ukraine, including battlefield developments and tactics
  • Western military responses to the war, and lessons they should learn
  • New weaponry built for and in response to the war 

Popular articles

  • Russia's invasion is showing the West war isn't just about having the best weapons — it's also about having more
  • Ukraine's Patriot kills of Russian planes and missiles have turned a US air-defense weapon with a troubled past into a hero
  • Ukraine's electronic warfare fight against Russian drones is so chaotic that its own are getting caught in the crossfire
  • Ukraine's defense industry says the fight against Russia has shown it that the West's approach to weapons is wrong
  • American sniper in Ukraine says he couldn't care less about expensive weapons like tanks. 'Give me artillery' and ammo, he says.

Email Sinéad at sbaker@businessinsider.com or find her on X.

sentences.zh: 被罢黜的乌克兰国防部长曾支持无人机战争。

blocks.html_en: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise dismissal of his reformist defense minister has sent shockwaves through the country, causing unease and anxiety as Kyiv enjoyed a wave of fresh momentum on the battlefield.

sentences.en: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise dismissal of his reformist defense minister has sent shockwaves through the country, causing unease and anxiety as Kyiv enjoyed a wave of fresh momentum on the battlefield.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰总统泽连斯基出人意料地解雇了改革派国防部长，这在全国范围内引起了轩然大波，在基辅战场上掀起了一股新的势头之际，引发了不安和焦虑。

blocks.html_en: Ukrainian defense industry sources described the removal of 35-year-old Mykhailo Fedorov last week, after just six months on the job, as a major disappointment for the country's powerhouse military-technology sector, expressing concerns about serious problems down the road.

sentences.en: Ukrainian defense industry sources described the removal of 35-year-old Mykhailo Fedorov last week, after just six months on the job, as a major disappointment for the country's powerhouse military-technology sector, expressing concerns about serious problems down the road.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰国防工业消息人士称，35岁的米哈伊洛·费多罗夫(Mykhailo Fedorov)上周在上任仅六个月后就被解除职务，这对该国强大的军事技术部门来说是一个重大失望，并对未来出现的严重问题表示担忧。

blocks.html_en: A Ukrainian defense tech company representative who asked only to be identified as Anton said that Fedorov "was driving innovations, visionary goals, and very brave practical operations that gave President Zelenskyy his cards."

sentences.en: A Ukrainian defense tech company representative who asked only to be identified as Anton said that Fedorov "was driving innovations, visionary goals, and very brave practical operations that gave President Zelenskyy his cards."
sentences.zh: 一位不愿透露姓名的乌克兰国防科技公司代表安东表示，费多罗夫“正在推动创新、实现远见卓识的目标和非常勇敢的实际行动，这为泽连斯基总统赢得了底牌”。

blocks.html_en: Anton said that Fedorov was proactive rather than reactive in his efforts to substantially overhaul Ukrainian weapons development, tactics, and doctrine, adding that "he was fulfilling today's needs and gaps while building tomorrow's relevance and superiority to ensure our victory."

sentences.en: Anton said that Fedorov was proactive rather than reactive in his efforts to substantially overhaul Ukrainian weapons development, tactics, and doctrine, adding that "he was fulfilling today's needs and gaps while building tomorrow's relevance and superiority to ensure our victory."
sentences.zh: 安东表示，费多罗夫在大幅改革乌克兰武器发展、战术和条令的努力中是积极主动的，而不是被动的，并补充说，“他正在满足今天的需求和差距，同时建立明天的相关性和优势，以确保我们的胜利。”

blocks.html_en: Fedorov, who previously served as Ukraine's minister for digital transformation and is known for his Silicon Valley-style approach to the war, was appointed by Zelenskyy in January to oversee the defense ministry.

sentences.zh: 费多罗夫曾担任乌克兰数字化转型部长，以其硅谷式的战争方式而闻名，他于一月份被泽连斯基任命负责监督国防部。

blocks.html_en: He quickly pushed to dramatically reform Ukraine's military, including placing greater emphasis on deploying drones and reshaping the contracting system. Fedorov also sought to overhaul the defense industry to increase development and make the procurement process more transparent and efficient.

sentences.en: He quickly pushed to dramatically reform Ukraine's military, including placing greater emphasis on deploying drones and reshaping the contracting system.
sentences.zh: 他迅速推动乌克兰军队的重大改革，包括更加重视部署无人机和重塑合同体系。

sentences.en: Fedorov also sought to overhaul the defense industry to increase development and make the procurement process more transparent and efficient.
sentences.zh: 费多罗夫还寻求彻底改革国防工业，以促进发展并使采购过程更加透明和高效。

blocks.html_en: Russia's invasion has turned Ukraine into a central hub for robotics and drone innovation. Hundreds of companies are building new technologies — from drones to electronic warfare devices — for the military, and many have their eyes set on future exports.

sentences.zh: 俄罗斯的入侵使乌克兰成为机器人和无人机创新的中心枢纽。

sentences.en: Hundreds of companies are building new technologies — from drones to electronic warfare devices — for the military, and many have their eyes set on future exports.

blocks.html_en: During Fedorov's tenure, Ukrainian firms enjoyed a much more transparent procurement process with the government, Anton said. He described collaborating with the defense ministry as a "professional and very productive" dialogue, "something we never could imagine before."

blocks.html_en: Since January, Ukrainian forces have been significantly ramping up their long-range drone strikes on Russian oil facilities and have used mid-range drones to hammer Russian logistics in the rear. sentences.en: Since January, Ukrainian forces have been significantly ramping up their long-range drone strikes on Russian oil facilities and have used mid-range drones to hammer Russian logistics in the rear.
sentences.zh: 自一月以来，乌克兰军队大幅加强了对俄罗斯石油设施的远程无人机打击，并使用中程无人机打击俄罗斯后方后勤。

sentences.en: The two campaigns have given Kyiv a sense of optimism and stymied Moscow's battlefield momentum while fueling its own.
sentences.zh: 这两场战役给基辅带来了乐观情绪，并阻碍了莫斯科的战场势头，同时助长了莫斯科的战场势头。

sentences.zh: 乌克兰国防公司高度评价费多罗夫创建更加透明的采购流程的能力。

sentences.en: Defense manufacturers see Ukraine's victories over the past few months as a testament to the drone-forward approach of Fedorov and his team.
sentences.zh: 国防制造商将乌克兰过去几个月的胜利视为费多罗夫及其团队的无人机前进方法的证明。

blocks.html_en: The CEO of robotic systems maker Ark Robotics said that "policies supporting rapid deployment, streamlined procurement, and continuous battlefield feedback created the conditions for rapid technological progress."

sentences.en: The CEO of robotic systems maker Ark Robotics said that "policies supporting rapid deployment, streamlined procurement, and continuous battlefield feedback created the conditions for rapid technological progress."
sentences.zh: 机器人系统制造商方舟机器人公司首席执行官表示，“支持快速部署、简化采购和持续战场反馈的政策为技术快速进步创造了条件。”

blocks.html_en: Losing the pace of innovation

sentences.en: Losing the pace of innovation
sentences.zh: 失去创新步伐

blocks.html_en: Fedorov's emphasis on rapid technological change and decentralized innovation sometimes clashed with the military's more conventional command structure under Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi. Zelenskyy said the rift between the two had become a problem.

sentences.en: Fedorov's emphasis on rapid technological change and decentralized innovation sometimes clashed with the military's more conventional command structure under Gen.
sentences.zh: 费多罗夫对快速技术变革和分散式创新的强调有时与将军领导下的军队更传统的指挥结构发生冲突。

sentences.en: Oleksandr Syrskyi.
sentences.zh: 亚历山大·西尔斯基。

sentences.en: Zelenskyy said the rift between the two had become a problem.
sentences.zh: 泽伦斯基表示，两人之间的裂痕已经成为一个问题。

blocks.html_en: Zelenskyy omitted Fedorov from his government in a major reshuffle last week. The former defense minister said he was pushed out, and while he declined a government advisory role, it's unclear whether he could stay on in another capacity.

sentences.en: Zelenskyy omitted Fedorov from his government in a major reshuffle last week.
sentences.zh: 泽伦斯基在上周的一次重大改组中将费多罗夫从政府中剔除。

sentences.en: The former defense minister said he was pushed out, and while he declined a government advisory role, it's unclear whether he could stay on in another capacity.
sentences.zh: 这位前国防部长表示，他被赶下了台，虽然他拒绝担任政府顾问，但目前尚不清楚他是否可以继续担任其他职务。

blocks.html_en: The dismissal caused confusion and anger among Ukrainian civilians and soldiers, and has stoked anxiety within the defense industry over the future of procurement and manufacturing priorities.

sentences.en: The dismissal caused confusion and anger among Ukrainian civilians and soldiers, and has stoked anxiety within the defense industry over the future of procurement and manufacturing priorities.
sentences.zh: 这次解雇引起了乌克兰平民和士兵的困惑和愤怒，并引发了国防工业内部对采购和制造优先事项的未来的焦虑。

sentences.en: Fedorov clashed with Syrskyi (right), who favored a more Soviet-style approach to the war.
sentences.zh: 费多罗夫与西尔斯基（右）发生冲突，西尔斯基更倾向于采取苏联式的战争方式。

sentences.en: Danylo Antoniuk/ Ukrinform/Future Publishing via Getty Images
sentences.zh: Danylo Antoniuk/Ukrinform/Future Publishing 来自 Getty Images

blocks.html_en: As defense minister, "Fedorov built a system where only equipment that actually worked and delivered results made it to the front," a Ukrainian manufacturer said, adding that "he created a competitive environment: manufacturers competed on quality, lowered their prices, and still met the front's real needs."

sentences.en: As defense minister, "Fedorov built a system where only equipment that actually worked and delivered results made it to the front," a Ukrainian manufacturer said, adding that "he created a competitive environment: manufacturers competed on quality, lowered their prices, and still met the front's real needs."
sentences.zh: 一位乌克兰制造商表示，作为国防部长，“费多罗夫建立了一个系统，只有真正发挥作用并交付成果的设备才能到达前线”，并补充说“他创造了一个竞争环境：制造商在质量上竞争，降低价格，但仍然满足前线的实际需求。”

blocks.html_en: They said they worry about a potential backslide to "manual, top-down control."

sentences.en: They said they worry about a potential backslide to "manual, top-down control."
sentences.zh: 他们表示，他们担心可能会倒退到“手动、自上而下的控制”。

blocks.html_en: Another Ukrainian manufacturer said they are "deeply concerned" as a producer, not only because of Fedorov's dismissal but also because Kyiv has gone through three defense ministers in a little over a year.

sentences.en: Another Ukrainian manufacturer said they are "deeply concerned" as a producer, not only because of Fedorov's dismissal but also because Kyiv has gone through three defense ministers in a little over a year.
sentences.zh: 另一家乌克兰制造商表示，作为生产商，他们“深感担忧”，不仅因为费多罗夫被解雇，还因为基辅在一年多的时间里更换了三任国防部长。

blocks.html_en: "This creates an unpredictable frame for work," they said. "Changing a minister and his team usually takes time to adjust all processes. This means that the adoption of necessary decisions can be slower for some time. Thus, this can affect our work as well."

sentences.en: "This creates an unpredictable frame for work," they said.
sentences.zh: “这创造了一个不可预测的工作框架，”他们说。

sentences.en: "Changing a minister and his team usually takes time to adjust all processes.
sentences.zh: “更换部长和他的团队通常需要时间来调整所有流程。

sentences.en: This means that the adoption of necessary decisions can be slower for some time.
sentences.zh: 这意味着必要决策的通过可能会在一段时间内放缓。

sentences.en: Thus, this can affect our work as well."
sentences.zh: 因此，这也会影响我们的工作。”

blocks.html_en: Zelenskyy said that he asked Maj. Gen. Yevhen Khmara, the acting head of Ukraine's SBU internal security agency, to lead the defense ministry in the interim.

sentences.en: Zelenskyy said that he asked Maj.
sentences.zh: 泽伦斯基说，他问了少校。

sentences.en: Gen.
Yevhen Khmara, the acting head of Ukraine's SBU internal security agency, to lead the defense ministry in the interim.
sentences.zh: 乌克兰 SBU 内部安全机构代理负责人叶夫亨·赫马拉 (Yevhen Khmara) 暂时担任国防部长。

sentences.en: Drones have become a key feature of Ukraine's strategy to repel the Russian invasion.
sentences.zh: 无人机已成为乌克兰击退俄罗斯入侵战略的一个关键特征。

sentences.en: Genya SAVILOV/AFP via Getty Images
sentences.zh: Genya SAVILOV/法新社，盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: Khmara previously led the SBU's elite Alpha unit, which has been the tip of the spear for Ukraine's deep strikes into Russia, so drone innovation may remain a priority for the defense ministry.

sentences.en: Khmara previously led the SBU's elite Alpha unit, which has been the tip of the spear for Ukraine's deep strikes into Russia, so drone innovation may remain a priority for the defense ministry.
sentences.zh: 赫马拉此前曾领导过 SBU 的精锐阿尔法部队，该部队一直是乌克兰对俄罗斯进行纵深打击的矛尖，因此无人机创新可能仍然是国防部的优先事项。

blocks.html_en: The Ark Robotics CEO said it remains to be seen whether Ukraine has the "institutional, strategic commitment to realize the same vision going forward and accelerate the changes" that Fedorov started.

sentences.en: The Ark Robotics CEO said it remains to be seen whether Ukraine has the "institutional, strategic commitment to realize the same vision going forward and accelerate the changes" that Fedorov started.
sentences.zh: 方舟机器人公司首席执行官表示，乌克兰是否具有费多罗夫发起的“制度和战略承诺，以实现未来的相同愿景并加速变革”，还有待观察。

blocks.html_en: Proper policies will enable defense innovation to move at the rapid pace required in a modern war, they said. "Our take is that being radical in implementing widespread use of unmanned systems is an existential question for Ukraine."

sentences.en: Proper policies will enable defense innovation to move at the rapid pace required in a modern war, they said.
sentences.zh: 他们表示，正确的政策将使国防创新能够按照现代战争所需的快速步伐发展。

sentences.en: "Our take is that being radical in implementing widespread use of unmanned systems is an existential question for Ukraine."
sentences.zh: “我们的看法是，激进地广泛使用无人系统对乌克兰来说是一个关乎生存的问题。”

blocks.html_en: Without Fedorov at the helm, Ukrainian defense manufacturers don't see their efforts completely collapsing. However, they are worried about a potential change in pace that could prove harmful at a pivotal moment in the war.

sentences.en: Without Fedorov at the helm, Ukrainian defense manufacturers don't see their efforts completely collapsing.
sentences.zh: 如果没有费多罗夫的掌舵，乌克兰国防制造商不会看到他们的努力完全崩溃。

sentences.en: However, they are worried about a potential change in pace that could prove harmful at a pivotal moment in the war.
sentences.zh: 然而，他们担心潜在的节奏变化可能会在战争的关键时刻造成损害。

blocks.html_en: "What happens to the industry, army, and country without Minister Fedorov?" Anton said. "Heaven will not fall on earth; the industry will work, as we all have a strong motivation to win this war, or at least not to lose."

sentences.en: "What happens to the industry, army, and country without Minister Fedorov?"
sentences.zh: “没有费多罗夫部长，工业、军队和国家会发生什么？”

sentences.en: Anton said.
sentences.zh: 安东说。

sentences.en: "Heaven will not fall on earth; the industry will work, as we all have a strong motivation to win this war, or at least not to lose."
sentences.zh: “天不会塌下来；这个行业将会发挥作用，因为我们都有强烈的动力去赢得这场战争，或者至少不要输掉。”

blocks.html_en: "We will continue doing our best, as well as the guys in the trenches," he said, but there's a risk that "we will lose the pace of development."

sentences.en: "We will continue doing our best, as well as the guys in the trenches," he said, but there's a risk that "we will lose the pace of development."
sentences.zh: 他说：“我们将继续竭尽全力，以及奋战在一线的人们。”但存在“我们将失去发展步伐”的风险。

blocks.html_en: Sinéad Baker is a Military and Defense Correspondent based in Business Insider's London bureau, writing about Russia's invasion of Ukraine and NATO actions.Sinéad most often covers soldiers' experiences, military strategy, battlefield developments, the defense industry's response, and geopolitical decisions that surround the war. She has reported from NATO’s frontlines and around Europe,  has interviewed multiple prime ministers and defense ministers, has appeared on BBC News and The Guardian's politics podcast, and has been cited by Congressional hearings.Sinéad has also extensively covered US politics and previously led Business Insider's breaking news coverage from London.Sinéad previously completed a master's degree in investigative journalism at City, University of London, and has written for The Guardian, The Observer, and TheJournal.ie. Sinéad is the former editor of the multi-award-winning The University Times in Dublin.Expertise

sentences.en: Sinéad Baker is a Military and Defense Correspondent based in Business Insider's London bureau, writing about Russia's invasion of Ukraine and NATO actions.Sinéad most often covers soldiers' experiences, military strategy, battlefield developments, the defense industry's response, and geopolitical decisions that surround the war.
sentences.zh: 西尼德·贝克 (Sinéad Baker) 是《商业内幕》伦敦分社的军事和国防记者，撰写有关俄罗斯入侵乌克兰和北约行动的文章。西尼德·贝克最常报道士兵的经历、军事战略、战场发展、国防工业的反应以及围绕战争的地缘政治决策。

sentences.en: She has reported from NATO’s frontlines and around Europe, has interviewed multiple prime ministers and defense ministers, has appeared on BBC News and The Guardian's politics podcast, and has been cited by Congressional hearings.Sinéad has also extensively covered US politics and previously led Business Insider's breaking news coverage from London.Sinéad previously completed a master's degree in investigative journalism at City, University of London, and has written for The Guardian, The Observer, and TheJournal.ie.
sentences.zh: 她曾在北约前线和欧洲各地进行报道，采访过多位首相和国防部长，曾出现在 BBC 新闻和《卫报》的政治播客上，并被国会听证会引用。Sinéad 还广泛报道了美国政治，并曾在伦敦领导《商业内幕》的突发新闻报道。Sinéad 此前在伦敦大学城市学院获得了调查新闻学硕士学位，并为《卫报》、《观察家报》和《卫报》撰稿。 TheJournal.ie。

sentences.en: Sinéad is the former editor of the multi-award-winning The University Times in Dublin.Expertise
sentences.zh: Sinéad 是都柏林屡获殊荣的《大学时报》的前编辑。专业知识

sentences.en: Western military responses to the war, and lessons they should learn
sentences.zh: 西方军事对战争的反应以及他们应该吸取的教训

sentences.en: New weaponry built for and in response to the war
sentences.zh: 为战争而建造的新武器

blocks.html_en: • Russia's invasion is showing the West war isn't just about having the best weapons — it's also about having more

sentences.en: Russia's invasion is showing the West war isn't just about having the best weapons — it's also about having more
sentences.zh: 俄罗斯的入侵表明，西方战争不仅仅在于拥有最好的武器，还在于拥有更多的武器

blocks.html_en: • Ukraine's Patriot kills of Russian planes and missiles have turned a US air-defense weapon with a troubled past into a hero

sentences.en: Ukraine's Patriot kills of Russian planes and missiles have turned a US air-defense weapon with a troubled past into a hero
sentences.zh: 乌克兰爱国者击落俄罗斯飞机和导弹，让过去麻烦不断的美国防空武器成为英雄

blocks.html_en: • Ukraine's electronic warfare fight against Russian drones is so chaotic that its own are getting caught in the crossfire

sentences.en: Ukraine's electronic warfare fight against Russian drones is so chaotic that its own are getting caught in the crossfire
sentences.zh: 乌克兰与俄罗斯无人机的电子战战斗非常混乱，以至于乌克兰自己也陷入了交火之中

blocks.html_en: • Ukraine's defense industry says the fight against Russia has shown it that the West's approach to weapons is wrong

sentences.en: Ukraine's defense industry says the fight against Russia has shown it that the West's approach to weapons is wrong
sentences.zh: 乌克兰国防工业表示，与俄罗斯的战斗表明西方对武器的态度是错误的

blocks.html_en: • American sniper in Ukraine says he couldn't care less about expensive weapons like tanks. 'Give me artillery' and ammo, he says.

sentences.en: American sniper in Ukraine says he couldn't care less about expensive weapons like tanks.
sentences.zh: 驻乌克兰的美国狙击手表示，他根本不在乎坦克等昂贵的武器。

sentences.en: 'Give me artillery' and ammo, he says.
sentences.zh: “给我大炮”和弹药，他说。

blocks.html_en: • Ukraine

sentences.en: Ukraine
sentences.zh: 乌克兰

blocks.html_en: • Russia

sentences.en: Russia
sentences.zh: 俄罗斯

content_html_zh:
乌克兰武器制造商担心，在他们的技术冠军被国防部赶出后，其武器制造商将面临代价高昂的经济放缓

作者：Jake Epstein 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 和 Sinéad Baker 您目前正在关注这位作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

乌克兰总统泽连斯基出人意料地解雇了改革派国防部长，这在全国范围内引起了轩然大波，在基辅战场上掀起了一股新的势头之际，引发了不安和焦虑。

乌克兰国防工业消息人士称，35岁的米哈伊洛·费多罗夫(Mykhailo Fedorov)上周在上任仅六个月后就被解除职务，这对该国强大的军事技术部门来说是一个重大失望，并对未来出现的严重问题表示担忧。

一位不愿透露姓名的乌克兰国防科技公司代表安东表示，费多罗夫“正在推动创新、实现远见卓识的目标和非常勇敢的实际行动，这为泽连斯基总统赢得了底牌”。

安东表示，费多罗夫在大幅改革乌克兰武器发展、战术和条令的努力中是积极主动的，而不是被动的，并补充说，“他正在满足今天的需求和差距，同时建立明天的相关性和优势，以确保我们的胜利。”

以改良主义的方式应对残酷的战争

费多罗夫曾担任乌克兰数字化转型部长，以其硅谷式的战争方式而闻名，他于一月份被泽连斯基任命负责监督国防部。

他迅速推动乌克兰军队的重大改革，包括更加重视部署无人机和重塑合同体系。 费多罗夫还寻求彻底改革国防工业，以促进发展并使采购过程更加透明和高效。

俄罗斯的入侵使乌克兰成为机器人和无人机创新的中心枢纽。 数百家公司正在为军队开发从无人机到电子战设备的新技术，许多公司都着眼于未来的出口。

安东说，在费多罗夫任职期间，乌克兰公司与政府的采购流程更加透明。 他将与国防部的合作描述为“专业且非常富有成效”的对话，“这是我们以前从未想象过的”。

他说，费多罗夫改变了国防工业，以应对不断变化的战场的挑战，这个战场变得越来越自治。

自一月以来，乌克兰军队大幅加强了对俄罗斯石油设施的远程无人机打击，并使用中程无人机打击俄罗斯后方后勤。 这两场战役给基辅带来了乐观情绪，并阻碍了莫斯科的战场势头，同时助长了莫斯科的战场势头。

国防制造商将乌克兰过去几个月的胜利视为费多罗夫及其团队的无人机前进方法的证明。

机器人系统制造商方舟机器人公司首席执行官表示，“支持快速部署、简化采购和持续战场反馈的政策为技术快速进步创造了条件。”

失去创新步伐

费多罗夫对快速技术变革和分散式创新的强调有时与将军领导下的军队更传统的指挥结构发生冲突。 亚历山大·西尔斯基。 泽伦斯基表示，两人之间的裂痕已经成为一个问题。

泽伦斯基在上周的一次重大改组中将费多罗夫从政府中剔除。 这位前国防部长表示，他被赶下了台，虽然他拒绝担任政府顾问，但目前尚不清楚他是否可以继续担任其他职务。

这次解雇引起了乌克兰平民和士兵的困惑和愤怒，并引发了国防工业内部对采购和制造优先事项的未来的焦虑。

一位乌克兰制造商表示，作为国防部长，“费多罗夫建立了一个系统，只有真正发挥作用并交付成果的设备才能到达前线”，并补充说“他创造了一个竞争环境：制造商在质量上竞争，降低价格，但仍然满足前线的实际需求。”

他们表示，他们担心可能会倒退到“手动、自上而下的控制”。

另一家乌克兰制造商表示，作为生产商，他们“深感担忧”，不仅因为费多罗夫被解雇，还因为基辅在一年多的时间里更换了三任国防部长。

“这创造了一个不可预测的工作框架，”他们说。 “更换部长和他的团队通常需要时间来调整所有流程。 这意味着必要决策的通过可能会在一段时间内放缓。 因此，这也会影响我们的工作。”

泽伦斯基说，他问了少校。 Gen. 乌克兰 SBU 内部安全机构代理负责人叶夫亨·赫马拉 (Yevhen Khmara) 暂时担任国防部长。

赫马拉此前曾领导过 SBU 的精锐阿尔法部队，该部队一直是乌克兰对俄罗斯进行纵深打击的矛尖，因此无人机创新可能仍然是国防部的优先事项。

方舟机器人公司首席执行官表示，乌克兰是否具有费多罗夫发起的“制度和战略承诺，以实现未来的相同愿景并加速变革”，还有待观察。

他们表示，正确的政策将使国防创新能够按照现代战争所需的快速步伐发展。 “我们的看法是，激进地广泛使用无人系统对乌克兰来说是一个关乎生存的问题。”

如果没有费多罗夫的掌舵，乌克兰国防制造商不会看到他们的努力完全崩溃。 然而，他们担心潜在的节奏变化可能会在战争的关键时刻造成损害。

“没有费多罗夫部长，工业、军队和国家会发生什么？” 安东说。 “天不会塌下来；这个行业将会发挥作用，因为我们都有强烈的动力去赢得这场战争，或者至少不要输掉。”

他说：“我们将继续竭尽全力，以及奋战在一线的人们。”但存在“我们将失去发展步伐”的风险。

西尼德·贝克 (Sinéad Baker) 是《商业内幕》伦敦分社的军事和国防记者，撰写有关俄罗斯入侵乌克兰和北约行动的文章。西尼德·贝克最常报道士兵的经历、军事战略、战场发展、国防工业的反应以及围绕战争的地缘政治决策。 她曾在北约前线和欧洲各地进行报道，采访过多位首相和国防部长，曾出现在 BBC 新闻和《卫报》的政治播客上，并被国会听证会引用。Sinéad 还广泛报道了美国政治，并曾在伦敦领导《商业内幕》的突发新闻报道。Sinéad 此前在伦敦大学城市学院获得了调查新闻学硕士学位，并为《卫报》、《观察家报》和《卫报》撰稿。 TheJournal.ie。 Sinéad 是都柏林屡获殊荣的《大学时报》的前编辑。专业知识

  • 乌克兰士兵的经历，包括战场发展和战术
  • 西方军事对战争的反应以及他们应该吸取的教训
  • 为战争而建造的新武器

热门文章

  • 俄罗斯的入侵表明，西方战争不仅仅在于拥有最好的武器，还在于拥有更多的武器
  • 乌克兰爱国者击落俄罗斯飞机和导弹，让过去麻烦不断的美国防空武器成为英雄
  • 乌克兰与俄罗斯无人机的电子战战斗非常混乱，以至于乌克兰自己也陷入了交火之中
  • 乌克兰国防工业表示，与俄罗斯的战斗表明西方对武器的态度是错误的
  • 驻乌克兰的美国狙击手表示，他根本不在乎坦克等昂贵的武器。 “给我大炮”和弹药，他说。
